{"query":"number Gaza patients awaiting medical treatment abroad 2026 last Rafah evacuation date","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1046,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.doctorswithoutborders.org/latest/deadly-delays-palestinians-waiting-medical-evacuation-gaza","title":"Deadly delays: The Palestinians waiting for medical evacuation from Gaza | Doctors Without Borders - USA","content":"There are now more than 18,500 Palestinians awaiting medical evacuation from Gaza, and more than 1 in 5 of them are children.
At least 1,000 patients have died while awaiting medical evacuation, according to the Ministry of Health, including one of our Doctors Without Borders/Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) colleagues, Abed El Hameed Qaradaya, who died while awaiting clearance for surgery that could have saved his life.
But this is only a drop in the ocean.
These are the stories of people waiting for a chance to survive.
I have three children who need evacuation, meaning they need medical treatment abroad.
I have a son who’s a cancer patient and is waiting to be evacuated too.
I see the referrals and evacuations around me, people telling me you want an evacuation in a month or two, our children have been waiting for months and years ..."},{"url":"https://arabcenterdc.org/resource/israels-restrictions-on-medical-evacuations-from-gaza-and-the-west-bank/","title":"Israel’s Restrictions on Medical Evacuations from Gaza and the West Bank","content":"Between February 2 and 10, 2026, 142 patients were evacuated, 91 of whom left through Rafah. At the same time, 223 Palestinians returned to Gaza through Rafah, ...Read more
“I have lost my last hope,” his mother told Haaretz.
In August 2023, almost 20,000 people left Gaza through Rafah, the highest number recorded since 2012.
The need for medical evacuations is significant, with an estimated 18,500 patients requiring health care abroad.
Still, only 4,895 medical evacuations were completed between October 7, 2023 and May 2024, when Israel seized Rafah, essentially halting movement of people in or out of Gaza, including for medical evacuations.
In February 2026, Israel approved a land registration measure that further dispossesses Palestinians from their land while facilitating settler land purchases in the West Bank.
Increasingly, Israel is banning humanitarian personnel from entering the West Bank altogether, and working to dismantle the humanitarian infrastructure remaining, including demolishing the UNRWA compound in East Jerusalem in late January 2026."},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2026/6/18/patients-die-in-gaza-waiting-for-medical-evacuations-israel-keeps-blocking","title":"Patients die in Gaza waiting for medical evacuations Israel keeps blocking | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera","content":"Rafa is one of more than 18,500 patients the World Health Organization (WHO) says require medical evacuation from Gaza for treatment unavailable in the Strip.
The October Gaza ceasefire explicitly required the resumption of medical evacuations from the Palestinian enclave.
On February 28, Israel closed the Rafah crossing again, and all medical evacuations have been suspended since.
Gaza’s Ministry of Health said six to 10 patients die each day waiting to travel abroad for treatment and roughly 1,200 have died since Israel seized the Rafah crossing in May 2024.
According to the WHO, 50 to 100 patients exited Gaza daily for medical care before October 2023."}]}

A group of patients and wounded Palestinians depart in ambulances and buses to be evacuated through the Rafah border crossing on February 15, 2026. More than 20,000 sick and wounded Palestinians in Gaza are waiting to travel abroad for treatment as Israel continues to operate the Rafah crossing border with Egypt under tight restrictions, the Palestinian Ministry of Health in Gaza said. In a statement on Sunday, the ministry said it is closely monitoring the continued partial and constrained operation of the crossing amid worsening health conditions in Gaza.
